subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II.

  List-I   List-II
A Sulphate I Pesticide
B Fluoride II Bending of bones
C Nicotine III Laxative effect
D Sodium arsinite IV Herbicide

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Match List I with List II.

  List-I   List-II
A Sulphate I Pesticide
B Fluoride II Bending of bones
C Nicotine III Laxative effect
D Sodium arsinite IV Herbicide

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
A-Sulphate - III (Laxative effect)

Sulfates are a large part of the haze pollution that irritates the lungs and affects your lung health.

B-Fluoride - II (Bending of bones)

Fluoride is a neurotoxin which, in high doses, can be harmful. Excessive exposure can lead to tooth discoloration and bone problems.

C-Nictoine - I (pesticides)

Nicotine has been used as a natural insecticide and is the archetype for the large range of synthetic neonicotinoid insecticides.

D-Sodium Arsinite - IV (herbicide)

Inorganic arsenicals (arsenic trioxide, sodium arsenite and lead arsenate) have been used as rodenticides and herbicides.

subject: Chemistry
q: The noble gas that does not occur in the atmosphere is;
question: The noble gas that does not occur in the atmosphere is;
solution: In the atmosphere, all the noble gases occur except radon. Out of all noble gases, argon is the major constituent. A decay product of radium is radon.

subject: Chemistry
q: Addition of phosphate fertilizers to water bodies causes:
question: Addition of phosphate fertilizers to water bodies causes:
options: Increase in amount of dissolved oxygen in water
solution: Addition of phosphate fertilizers or other fertilizers to water bodies causes enrichment in nutrients, this phenomenon is called Eutrophication. Increase in nutrients enhanced growth of algae (vegetation) which cause consume of dissolved oxygen. Hence decrease in quantity of dissolved oxygen, effects the life of aqua-animals or other vegetation. So Eutrophication is a types of water pollution.

subject: Chemistry
q: The photochemical smog does not generally contain
question: The photochemical smog does not generally contain
solution:
The major components of photochemical smog are nitrogen oxides, ozone O3, PAN (peroxyacetylnitrate), and chemical compounds that contain the-CHO group (aldehydes). PAN and aldehydes can cause eye irritation and plant damage if their concentrations are sufficiently high.

Classical smog occurs in a cool, humid climate. Its components include smoke, fog and sulphur dioxide. It is reducing in nature.

subject: Chemistry
q: The water having more dissolved <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is :
question: The water having more dissolved <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is :
solution: On heating concentration of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> in water decreases. So boiling water and water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>80</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> having less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration. Polluted water also having less <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration. So water at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>°</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> is having maximum <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> concentration.

subject: Chemistry
q: The primary pollutant that leads to photochemical smog is:
question: The primary pollutant that leads to photochemical smog is:
solution: Nitrogen oxides and hydrocarbons (unburnt fuel) are primary pollutant that leads to photochemical smog.

subject: Chemistry
q: The pH of rain water is approximately:
question: The pH of rain water is approximately:
solution:
Normal, clean rain has a pH value of between 5.0 and 5.6, which is slightly acidic. However, when rain combines with sulfur dioxide or nitrogen oxides produced from power plants and automobiles the rain becomes much more acidic. Typical acid rain has a pH value of 4.0.

Rain water becomes acidic because gases present in environment are dissolved, so it's pH will be less than 7.

question: Biochemical Oxygen Demand <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is the amount of oxygen required (in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> ):
options: for sustaining life in a water body.
by bacteria to break-down organic waste in a certain volume of a water sample.

for the photochemical breakdown of waste present in <math><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math> volume of a water body
by anaerobic bacteria to breakdown. inorganic waste present in a water body.
solution: The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical oxygen Demand (BOD).

subject: Chemistry
q:
Assertion: Ozone is destroyed by CFCs in the upper stratosphere.
Reason: Ozone holes increase the amount of UV radiation reaching the earth.
question:
Assertion: Ozone is destroyed by CFCs in the upper stratosphere.
Reason: Ozone holes increase the amount of UV radiation reaching the earth.
options: Assertion and reason are correct, but the reason is not the explanation for the assertion.
The assertion is false, but the reason is correct.
Assertion and reason are both correct, and the reason is the correct explanation for the assertion.
solution:
When CFCs react with ozone, then it breaks the molecule of ozone gas which results in ozone layer depletion. Ozone layer protect us from harmful ultraviolet (UV) radiations coming from the sun. Thus, ozone holes increase the amount of UV radiation reaching the earth.

The upper stratosphere consists of ozone O3, which protect us from harmful ultraviolet UV radiations coming from sun.

subject: Chemistry
q: In stratosphere most of the ozone formation is assisted by :
question: In stratosphere most of the ozone formation is assisted by :
solution:
Ozone in the stratosphere is a product of UV radiations acting on dioxygen O2 molecules.

O2( g)⟶UVO(g)+O(g)

O(g)+O2( g)⇌UVO3( g)

subject: Chemistry
q: The concentration of dissolved Oxygen in water for growth of fish should be more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> and Biochemical Oxygen Demand in clean water should be less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> in ppm are, respectively.
question: The concentration of dissolved Oxygen in water for growth of fish should be more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math> and Biochemical Oxygen Demand in clean water should be less than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>¯</mo></munder><mo> </mo><mi>ppm</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> in ppm are, respectively.
solution: The concentration of dissolved oxygen in water is very important for aquatic life. If the concentration of dissolved oxygen of water is below 6 ppm, the growth of fish gets inhibited. The amount of oxygen required by bacteria to break down the organic matter present in a certain volume of a sample of water, is called Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD). The amount of BOD in the water is a measure of the amount of organic material in the water, in terms of how much oxygen will be required to break it down biologically. Clean water would have BOD value of less than 5 ppm whereas highly polluted water could have a BOD value of 17 ppm or more.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements :

Statement-I : If BOD is 4  ppm and dissolved oxygen is 8  ppm , then it is a good quality water.

Statement-II : If the concentration of zinc and nitrate salts are 5 ppm each, then it can be a good quality water.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements :

Statement-I : If BOD is 4  ppm and dissolved oxygen is 8  ppm , then it is a good quality water.

Statement-II : If the concentration of zinc and nitrate salts are 5 ppm each, then it can be a good quality water.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both the statements I and II are incorrect
Both the statements I and II are correct
solution:
Drinking water has a BOD level of 1 - 2 ppm. When the BOD value of water is in the range 3 - 5 ppm, the water is moderately clean. Polluted water has a BOD value in the range of 6 - 9 ppm. Healthy water should generally have dissolved oxygen concentrations above 6.5-8 mg/L

Drinking water with concentrations of nitrate (measured as nitrate-nitrogen) below 10 mg of nitrate per liter of water (mg/L) is considered safe. Permissible limit of Zn in potable water is 5 ppm.

question:
The green house gas/es is (are):

(A) Carbon dioxide

(B) Oxygen

(C) Water vapour

(D) Methane

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
solution:
Greenhouse effect, a warming of Earth's surface and troposphere (the lowest layer of the atmosphere) caused by the presence of water vapour, carbon dioxide, methane, and certain other gases in the air. Of those gases, known as greenhouse gases, water vapour has the largest effect.

The green house gases are CO2, H2Ovapour and CH4.

question: The type of pollution that gets increased during the daytime and in the presence of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is :
solution:
In presence of ozone O3, oxidising smog gets increased during the daytime because automobiles and factories produce main components of the photochemical smog (oxidising smog) results from the action of sunlight on unsaturated hydrocarbon and nitrogen oxide.

Ozone is a strong oxidising agent and can react with the unburnt hydrocarbons in the polluted air to produce chemicals.

subject: Chemistry
q: Which of these reactions is not a part of breakdown of ozone in stratosphere?
question: Which of these reactions is not a part of breakdown of ozone in stratosphere?
solution:
Chlorofluorocarbon compounds (CFCs) are non-reactive, non-flammable molecules that are used in refrigerators, air conditioners, plastics, and electronic industries. Once released CFCs mix with atmospheric gases and reach the stratosphere, where they are decomposed by UV radiations.

CF2Cl2g→uvCl⊙g+C⊙F2Clg

The chlorine radical released will react with ozone.

Cl⊙g+O3g→O2g+ClOg⊙

The Cl⊙O will generate chlorine radicals again

ClOg⊙+Og→O2g+Cl⊙

subject: Chemistry
q: The statement that is not true about ozone is :
question: The statement that is not true about ozone is :
options: in the stratosphere, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CFC</mi></math>s release chlorine free radicals <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>CI</mi></mfenced></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"></math>which reacts with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> to give chlorine
in the atmosphere, it is depleted by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CFC</mi></math>s.
in the stratosphere, it forms a protective shield against UV radiation.

It is a toxic gas and its reaction with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NO</mi></math> gives <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>
solution:
In presence of sunlight CFC’s molecule divides & release chlorine free radical, which react with ozone give chlorine monoxide radical (ClO) and oxygen

CF2Cl2g⟶UVC˙lg+C˙F2Clg

Cl*(g)+O3(g)⟶ClO*(g)+O2(g)

ClO∙(g)+O(g)⟶Cl'(g)+O2(g)

q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement II : The fuel obtained from plastic waste is lead free.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : The non bio-degradable fly ash and slag from steel industry can be used by cement industry.

Statement II : The fuel obtained from plastic waste is lead free.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
solution:
(I) The disposal of non-degradable industrial solid wastes, if not done by a proper and suitable method, may cause serious threat to the environment. New innovations have led to different uses of waste material. Nowadays, fly ash and slag from the steel industry are utilised by the cement industry.

(II) Fuel obtained from plastic waste has high octane rating. It contains no lead and it is known as green fuel.

Both statement (I) & (II) are correct.

subject: Chemistry
q: BOD stands for:
question: BOD stands for:
solution:
Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) is also called Biological Oxygen Demand.

It is the amount of dissolved oxygen needed (i.e. demanded) by aerobic biological organisms to break down organic material present in a given water sample at certain temperature over a specific time period

subject: Chemistry
q: Water sample is reported to be highly polluted if BOD (Biological Oxygen Demand) value of sample becomes
question: Water sample is reported to be highly polluted if BOD (Biological Oxygen Demand) value of sample becomes
solution: Water sample is reported to be highly polluted if BOD value of sample is more than 17 ppm.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two Statements :

Statement I : Classical smog occurs in cool humid climate. It is a reducing mixture of smoke, fog and sulphur dioxide.

Statement II : Photochemical smog has components, ozone, nitric oxide, acrolein, formaldehyde, PAN etc.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two Statements :

Statement I : Classical smog occurs in cool humid climate. It is a reducing mixture of smoke, fog and sulphur dioxide.

Statement II : Photochemical smog has components, ozone, nitric oxide, acrolein, formaldehyde, PAN etc.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
solution:
Classical smog occurs in cool humid climate. It is a mixture of smoke, fog and sulphur dioxide. Chemically it is a reducing mixture and so it is also called as reducing smog.

Photochemical smog occurs in warm, dry and sunny climate. The main components of the photochemical smog result from the action of sunlight on unsaturated hydrocarbons and nitrogen oxides produced by automobiles and factories. Photochemical smog has high concentration of oxidising agents and is, therefore, called as oxidising smog.

Ozone is a toxic gas and both NO2 and O3 are strong oxidising agents and can react with the unburnt hydrocarbons in the polluted air to produce chemicals such as formaldehyde, acrolein and peroxyacetyl nitrate (PAN).

Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
solution:
Species Maximum allowed concentration in ppm in drinking water
A. F- 2
B. SO42- 500
C. NO3- 50
D. Zn  5

F– ion concentration above 2 ppm causes brown mottling of teeth. At the same time, excess fluoride (over 10 ppm) causes harmful effect to bones and teeth.

Excessive sulphate (>500 ppm) in drinking water causes laxative effect, otherwise at moderate levels it is harmless.

The maximum limit of nitrate in drinking water is 50 ppm. Excess nitrate in drinking water can cause disease such as methemoglobinemia (‘blue baby’ syndrome).

subject: Chemistry
q: Which one of the following substances used in dry cleaning is a better strategy to control environmental pollution?
question: Which one of the following substances used in dry cleaning is a better strategy to control environmental pollution?
solution: CO2 is used in dry cleaning is a better strategy to control environmental pollution. Liquid carbon dioxide is better to replace conventional halogenated solvents which are potentially carcinogenic. These detergents are developed in a way that one end of the molecule is soluble in non-polar substances like grease, oil stains and the other end dissolves in liquid carbon dioxide.
